title: Avoiding insert errors
summary: Learn how to avoid common errors when inserting data into tables by using explicit column lists, understanding required columns, and handling NULLs and defaults safely.

sketchBlocks.title: Why insert errors happen
sketchBlocks.bodyMarkdown: When inserting data into a table, errors often occur if you leave out required columns, mismatch column order, or provide the wrong data types. For example, the `inventory_items` table requires `name`, `category`, `price`, `status`, `is_test`, and `last_updated` to be provided, unless a default is set. Forgetting to specify a NOT NULL column or mixing up the order can cause your insert to fail.

sketchBlocks.title: Safer inserts with explicit columns
sketchBlocks.bodyMarkdown:
It's best practice to always specify the columns you are inserting into. For example:

```sql
INSERT INTO inventory_items (name, category, price, status, is_test, last_updated)
VALUES ('New Keychain', 'Accessories', 3.50, 'active', 0, '2026-02-10');
```

This makes your intent clear and avoids errors if the table structure changes.

sketchBlocks.title: Handling NULLs and defaults
sketchBlocks.bodyMarkdown: Some columns allow NULL values or have defaults. For example, `notes` is nullable, and `status` has a default of `'draft'`. If you omit `notes`, it will be NULL. If you omit `status`, it will default to `'draft'` unless you specify otherwise. Understanding which columns are required, nullable, or have defaults helps you avoid insert errors.

quizDraft.title: Insert a new inventory item safely
quizDraft.prompt:
Write an INSERT statement to add a new row to `inventory_items` with the following values:
- name: 'Desk Organizer'
- category: 'Accessories'
- price: 8.25
- status: (use the default)
- is_test: 0
- notes: leave as NULL
- last_updated: '2026-02-10'

Use an explicit column list and do not provide a value for `status` or `notes`.
quizDraft.hint: List only the columns you are providing values for. Omit columns that should use their default or be NULL.

help.concept: Specifying columns in your INSERT statement helps avoid errors and lets the database use defaults or NULLs for omitted columns.
help.hint_1: Include only the columns you want to set. The database will use the default for `status` and NULL for `notes`.
help.hint_2: Your column list should match the values you provide. Do not mention `status` or `notes` in the column list.

quizDraft.solutionCode:
INSERT INTO inventory_items (name, category, price, is_test, last_updated)
VALUES ('Desk Organizer', 'Accessories', 8.25, 0, '2026-02-10');

quizDraft.title: Insert with all required columns
quizDraft.prompt:
Write an INSERT statement that adds a new inventory item with these details:
- name: 'Ceramic Coaster'
- category: 'Kitchen'
- price: 5.00
- status: 'active'
- is_test: 0
- notes: 'Limited edition'
- last_updated: '2026-02-11'

Make sure all NOT NULL columns are included.
quizDraft.hint: Check which columns are NOT NULL and provide values for them. Include optional columns if you want to set them.

help.concept: NOT NULL columns must always be given a value in your INSERT statement, unless they have a default.
help.hint_1: Include all columns except for the primary key if it's auto-incremented.
help.hint_2: Don't forget to include `notes` since you want to set it, even though it's nullable.

quizDraft.solutionCode:
INSERT INTO inventory_items (name, category, price, status, is_test, notes, last_updated)
VALUES ('Ceramic Coaster', 'Kitchen', 5.00, 'active', 0, 'Limited edition', '2026-02-11');

quizDraft.title: Insert with NULL for optional column
quizDraft.prompt: Add a new row to `inventory_items` for a product called 'Sample Magnet' in the 'Decor' category, priced at 2.75. Set status to 'draft', is_test to 1, and last_updated to '2026-02-12'. Leave `notes` as NULL.
quizDraft.hint: Include all NOT NULL columns and set `notes` to NULL explicitly or omit it from the column list.

help.concept: You can omit nullable columns from your column list, or explicitly insert NULL for them.
help.hint_1: If you want `notes` to be NULL, you can either leave it out or set it to NULL.
help.hint_2: If you include `notes` in the column list, make sure to use the value NULL.

quizDraft.solutionCode:
INSERT INTO inventory_items (name, category, price, status, is_test, last_updated)
VALUES ('Sample Magnet', 'Decor', 2.75, 'draft', 1, '2026-02-12');

quizDraft.title: Which actions help avoid insert errors?
quizDraft.prompt: Select all actions that help prevent errors when inserting data into a table.
quizDraft.hint: Think about practices that make your intent clear and match the table structure.

help.concept: Using explicit column lists, providing all required values, and understanding defaults/NULLs help avoid insert errors.
help.hint_1: Consider what happens if you omit a required column or mix up the order.
help.hint_2: Actions that clarify which columns get which values are safest.

options.a: Always specify the column list in your INSERT statement.
options.b: Provide values for all NOT NULL columns.
options.c: Rely on the default order of columns without specifying them.
options.d: Omit columns that have defaults or allow NULLs if you don't want to set them.

quizDraft.title: Default values in inserts
quizDraft.prompt: When you omit a column with a DEFAULT value in your INSERT statement, the database will use the ____ for that column.
quizDraft.hint: Think about what the database does when you don't provide a value for a column with a default.

help.concept: If a column has a DEFAULT value and you omit it from your INSERT, the database fills in the default.
help.hint_1: The database doesn't leave the column empty; it uses something specified in the table definition.
help.hint_2: Check the table schema for the keyword that tells the database what to use if no value is provided.

quizDraft.template: When you omit a column with a DEFAULT value in your INSERT statement, the database will use the ____ for that column.
